findings.description: This rule conflates two distinct requirements from the same bullet: (1) the textual attribution format/styling for patient quotes ('- [Name], LISRAYA patient', italic/distinguished styling) which is copy_editorial, and (2) a pairing requirement with 'warm lifestyle imagery', which is a visual/assembly-level constraint about accompanying imagery, not copy editorial styling. Bundling them under one copy_editorial rule obscures the imagery-pairing obligation and risks it being dropped or mis-governed under editorial review only.

new_rules.statement: Patient quotes must be attributed as '- [Name], LISRAYA patient' with italic or otherwise visually distinguished styling.
Patient quotes must be paired with warm lifestyle imagery.

findings.description: The source unit cites a specific governance reference (§1.2) tying the footnote-marker requirement to a numbered policy/regulatory clause. The clustered rule does not appear to carry this citation/governance facet, so downstream consumers lose traceability to the source policy section that justifies the hard_constraint hardness.

payload.governance_note: Add explicit citation field referencing §1.2 as the sourcing clause for footnote marker requirement.

findings.description: The unit specifies two sub-requirements: (a) clinical claims require footnote markers (†), and (b) the footnote text itself must be 'clearly separated and noticeably smaller' — a typographic/layout constraint distinct from the marker-insertion requirement. Currently folded into one copy_editorial rule, the typographic sizing/separation requirement risks being scoped only to 'editorial' review rather than also flagged for layout/design QA, understating its cross-functional scope.

payload.scope_note: Extend scope to include layout/design QA in addition to copy_editorial, since footnote text separation and smaller sizing is a typographic/layout requirement, not purely editorial.
